rationale: A single external IP (23.93.242.200) generated 23 inbound TCP connections to RDP port 3389 on host-01.corp.local over approximately 5.5 minutes (A0 through EV-022), with each connection using a sequentially incrementing source port — a pattern strongly indicative of an automated RDP brute-force or credential-stuffing attack. The source IP (23.93.242.200) is a public internet address with no apparent legitimate business justification for direct RDP access to an internal host. The high connection frequency, especially the burst seen in EV-011 through EV-022 where multiple connections occur within seconds of each other, further supports automated attack tooling rather than a human user. Immediate action is warranted to block the source IP, review RDP authentication logs for successful logins, and assess whether the host should be isolated pending investigation.
